verses.text: Ye are our epistle, 'written not with ink, but with the Spirit,' in the heart. Living letters. Their changed lives commend his ministry.
Ye are our epistle, 'written not with ink, but with the Spirit,' in the heart. Living letters. Their changed lives commend his ministry.
Ye are our epistle, 'written not with ink, but with the Spirit,' in the heart. Living letters. Their changed lives commend his ministry.
Our sufficiency is of God, ministers of the new testament; 'the letter killeth, but the spirit giveth life.' Spirit and life. The law condemns; the gospel makes alive.
Our sufficiency is of God, ministers of the new testament; 'the letter killeth, but the spirit giveth life.' Spirit and life. The law condemns; the gospel makes alive.
Our sufficiency is of God, ministers of the new testament; 'the letter killeth, but the spirit giveth life.' Spirit and life. The law condemns; the gospel makes alive.
If the ministration of death was glorious, much more 'the ministration of the spirit.' Greater glory. The new covenant outshines the old.
If the ministration of death was glorious, much more 'the ministration of the spirit.' Greater glory. The new covenant outshines the old.
If the ministration of death was glorious, much more 'the ministration of the spirit.' Greater glory. The new covenant outshines the old.
What was glorious 'had no glory... by reason of the glory that excelleth.' Surpassed. The old fades before the brighter new.
What was glorious 'had no glory... by reason of the glory that excelleth.' Surpassed. The old fades before the brighter new.
Moses veiled his face; their minds are blinded, the veil remaining in reading. Veiled hearts. Without Christ, the Scriptures stay hidden.
Moses veiled his face; their minds are blinded, the veil remaining in reading. Veiled hearts. Without Christ, the Scriptures stay hidden.
Moses veiled his face; their minds are blinded, the veil remaining in reading. Veiled hearts. Without Christ, the Scriptures stay hidden.
Moses veiled his face; their minds are blinded, the veil remaining in reading. Veiled hearts. Without Christ, the Scriptures stay hidden.
When one turns to the Lord, the veil is removed; 'we all... beholding... the glory of the Lord, are changed into the same image.' Transformed. Gazing on Christ makes us like Him.
